section_header: approach 1: benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings

parent.policy_statement: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
parent.verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 1:
## BENCHMARK, RETUNE, AND RETROFIT EXISTING BUILDINGS
parent.section_header: APPROACH 1: BENCHMARK, RETUNE, AND RETROFIT EXISTING BUILDINGS
parent.extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple specific targets and actions listed beneath it; serves as umbrella for sub-policies.

subs.policy_statement: Benchmark 1.3 billion square feet of communitywide buildings by 2026.
subs.verbatim_text: - Benchmark 1.3 billion square feet in communitywide buildings by 2026
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 1: BENCHMARK, RETUNE, AND RETROFIT EXISTING BUILDINGS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline under Approach 1.

subs.policy_statement: Retrofit 167,500 homes by 2030 to reduce energy costs 28%, prioritizing Low- to Moderate-Income (LMI) homes.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Retrofit 167,500 homes to reduce energy costs 28%, prioritizing Low- to Moderate-Income (LMI) homes by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 1: BENCHMARK, RETUNE, AND RETROFIT EXISTING BUILDINGS
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified retrofit target, performance outcome (28% cost reduction), equity prioritization, and deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Adopt a Building Performance Ordinance to ensure benchmarking and retuning of large existing buildings community-wide.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Adopt a Building Performance Ordinance to ensure benchmarking and retuning of large existing buildings community-wide
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 1: BENCHMARK, RETUNE, AND RETROFIT EXISTING BUILDINGS
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory mechanism ('Adopt...Ordinance') stated as an action under Approach 1.

subs.verbatim_text: -   Expand weatherization assistance through repairs and upgrades to improve energy efficiency, safety, and comfort in Low to Moderate Income (LMI) housing
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 1: BENCHMARK, RETUNE, AND RETROFIT EXISTING BUILDINGS
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete program expansion action targeting energy efficiency in LMI housing.

subs.policy_statement: Create a Quality Assurance Verifier position to support the guidelines and applicability of the Building Performance Ordinance.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Create a Quality Assurance Verifier position to support the guidelines and applicability of Building Performance Ordinance
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit staffing/resource action to implement an ordinance.

subs.policy_statement: Offer monetary and/or non-monetary incentives to promote building retrofits, starting with LMI buildings.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Offer  monetary  and/or  non-monetary  incentives  to  promote  building  retrofits,  starting  with LMI buildings
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 1: Benchmark, retune, and retrofit existing buildings to reduce emissions from energy use in buildings.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (incentives) aimed at driving retrofits, with prioritization.

subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to update a named plan.

section_header: approach 2: expand on-site and off-site renewable energy generation

parent.policy_statement: Approach 2: Expand on-site and off-site renewable energy generation.
parent.verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 2:
## EXPAND ON-SITE AND OFF-SITE
## RENEWABLE ENERGY GENERATION

parent.extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple quantified targets/actions listed beneath it.

subs.policy_statement: Install 61,725 kW of solar energy by 2030 on County buildings, land, and water (equivalent to 7,498 homes' electricity use for one year).
subs.verbatim_text: -   Install 61,725 kW of solar energy by 2030 on County buildings, land, and water equivalent to 7,498 homes' electricity use for one year
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 2: Expand on-site and off-site renewable energy generation.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 2: EXPAND ON-SITE AND OFF-SITE RENEWABLE ENERGY GENERATION
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified capacity target with deadline and specified siting (County buildings/land/water).

subs.verbatim_text: -   Install  794,000  kW  of  solar  energy  by  2030  on  commercial  and  residential  buildings, equivalent to 104,014 homes' electricity use for one year

subs.policy_statement: Complete installation of solar on County buildings as identified in Solar Feasibility Study Phase 2.

subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 2: Expand on-site and off-site renewable energy generation.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete implementation commitment tied to a named feasibility study phase.

subs.verbatim_text: -   Establish  the  Solar  Energy  Loan  Fund program  and  similar  financial  mechanisms in Miami-Dade County

subs.verbatim_text: -   Require new County buildings to be solar ready for the County to lead by example

subs.extraction_rationale: Specific requirement for new County buildings (policy/standard).

subs.policy_statement: Cut soft costs for installing on-site solar energy.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Cut  soft  costs  for  installing  on-site  solar energy
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 2: Expand on-site and off-site renewable energy generation.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific action to reduce non-hardware costs to enable deployment.

subs.policy_statement: Execute an agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy's National Renewable Energy Laboratory (NREL) to pursue mutually beneficial projects to reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Execute  an  agreement with the United States Department of Energy's National Renewable Energy Laboratory to pursue mutually  beneficial  projects  to  reduce greenhouse gas emissions.
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 2: Expand on-site and off-site renewable energy generation.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific institutional agreement/action aimed at emissions reductions.

subs.policy_statement: Reduce energy use of new buildings 20% by 2030 below 2020 levels.
subs.verbatim_text: - Reduce energy use of new buildings 20% by 2030 below 2020 levels

subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified efficiency target with baseline year and deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Ensure all new County buildings will be Ultra-Low Energy by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: - All new County buildings will be Ultra-Low Energy by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 3: Build ultra-low energy buildings.

subs.policy_statement: Identify steps to transition to a net-zero ready code.

subs.policy_statement: Offer monetary and/or non-monetary incentives to promote construction of ULE or NZE buildings.

section_header: approach 5: expand and protect green and blue spaces

parent.policy_statement: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
parent.verbatim_text:
## APPROACH 5:
## EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
parent.section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
parent.extraction_rationale: Named approach with multiple targets/actions under it.

subs.policy_statement: Increase community-wide tree canopy to 30% coverage by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Expand community-wide tree canopy to 30% coverage by 2030.
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified canopy target with deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Ensure all County facilities within the Urban Development Boundary attain at least 30% average canopy coverage and facilities outside the UDB attain at least 50% average canopy coverage by 2030.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Ensure that all County facilities within the UDB (per 2013 delineation) shall attain an average of  at  least  30%  canopy  coverage  and  all  County  facilities  outside  the  Urban  Development Boundary (UDB) shall attain an average of at least 50% canopy coverage by 2030
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
subs.extraction_rationale: Binding 'shall' language with quantified canopy coverage targets and deadline.

subs.policy_statement: Double the total non-wetland acreage of natural habitat in preservation.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Double the total non-wetland acreage of natural habitat in preservation
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear quantified conservation action (doubling preserved non-wetland habitat).

subs.policy_statement: Develop a mitigation policy, based on area impacted, to ensure County-approved development results in a net increase to green infrastructure by the development's completion date.
subs.verbatim_text: -   Develop  a  mitigation  policy,  based  on  area  impacted,  to  ensure  that  County-approved development results in a net increase to green infrastructure by the development's completion date
subs.parent_policy_name: Approach 5: Expand and protect green and blue spaces.
subs.section_header: APPROACH 5: EXPAND AND PROTECT GREEN AND BLUE SPACES
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific policy development commitment with defined intended outcome (net increase in green infrastructure).

section_header: letter from mayor daniella levine cava

individual.policy_statement: Implement the Climate Action Strategy (CAS) to cut Miami-Dade communitywide greenhouse gas emissions 50% by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
individual.verbatim_text: Officially launched on Oct. 27, 2021, this plan describes work taking place on multiple fronts to cut MiamiDade's greenhouse gas emissions 50 percent by 2030 from 2019 levels, with the goal of achieving net zero by 2050.
individual.section_header: LETTER FROM MAYOR DANIELLA LEVINE CAVA
individual.extraction_rationale: Contains quantified emissions-reduction targets with deadlines (50% by 2030; net zero by 2050) tied to an implemented strategy.

section_header: seven approaches to cut emissions 50% by 2030

individual.policy_statement: Report progress on the Climate Action Strategy annually and update the Strategy every five years.
individual.verbatim_text: While a gap remains to reach the goal of a 50% cut by 2030, progress will be reported on an annual basis and the Strategy will be updated every five-years to reflect technological advances and new state and federal energy and transportation policies.
individual.section_header: SEVEN APPROACHES TO CUT EMISSIONS 50% BY 2030
individual.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitments to recurring reporting and scheduled updates (annual reporting; five-year updates).

individual.policy_statement: Convene meetings with South Florida governments, utilities, schools, businesses, and institutions to identify solutions to close the emissions gap toward the 2030 target.
individual.verbatim_text: Action: To identify solutions to close the emissions gap, Mayor Daniella Levine Cava will convene meetings with multiple South Florida governments, utility providers, schools, businesses, and institutions that have set aggressive greenhouse gas reduction goals.
individual.extraction_rationale: Specific, actor-assigned intervention ('will convene meetings') to develop solutions for the quantified emissions gap.

individual.policy_statement: Track progress toward Climate Action Strategy targets through greenhouse gas inventories conducted every two years, and update the Strategy in five years.
individual.verbatim_text: Progress towards specific targets will be tracked through greenhouse gas inventories conducted every two years. In five years, the Climate Action Strategy will be updated to incorporate new technology, funding opportunities, and community identified solutions.
individual.extraction_rationale: Defines a concrete monitoring mechanism and frequency (biennial inventories) and a timed update (in five years).

individual.policy_statement: Build a HyBrTec biosolids-to-hydrogen pilot plant to test technology converting wastewater biosolids to green hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and heat, supported by a $1.15 million Florida DEP grant.
individual.verbatim_text: Miami-Dade County's Water and Sewer Department is building a HyBrTec biosolids-hydrogen pilot plant, supported by a $1.15 million Florida Department of Environmental Protection grant. The plant will test a new technology that convert biosolids from wastewater treatment to green hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and heat.
individual.extraction_rationale: Specific project investment with funding amount and defined technology/purpose.
